tldr: 5 of your 7 live Meta ads are scoring high-fatigue. At your current pace, expect CPM creep within 8–14 days. Below: the per-ad breakdown, the one ad we'd scale, the one we'd kill today, and 3 replacement concepts written in your voice.

benchmark.context: Top fashion brands at your spend tier refresh winning hooks every 22 days. Yours are clustering at 12 — meaning you're feeding new creative against a cooling audience signal.

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: Verbatim duplicate of two other live ads — three-way auction overlap
When Meta sees identical copy, it consolidates delivery toward one — wasting the other two
Same start date as duplicates, so Meta cannot stagger them

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: Verbatim body copy duplicate of Ad #1 and Ad #3
Triple-overlap on copy means highest auction self-cannibalization risk in the set
Same launch cluster, same landing — no diversification

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
drivers: 'Evening elegance, reimagined' copy is duplicated across 3 of your 7 ads
Title 'New Arrivals' appears in 6 of 7 ads — Meta deduplicates lookalike creative
All 7 ads point to the same /collections/new-in landing — no PDP testing

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
drivers: Exact body copy duplicate of Ad #5 — competing for the same auction win
Title + CTA + landing identical to 5 other active ads
No video, no carousel, no UGC variant in the entire ad set

ads.headline: New Arrivals
ads.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night. Discover our SS26 collection.
drivers: 'Elegance made easy' body copy reused in 3 of 7 ads
Identical title + CTA + landing as 5 other active ads
All ads launched within a 48-hour window — they will fatigue together

ads_compact.headline: New Arrivals
ads_compact.body: Elegance made easy, from day to night...

ad_to_scale.headline: Discover SS26
ad_to_scale.body: Celebrate with confidence – new arrivals are crafted for life's special moments.
ad_to_scale.why: It's the only ad with unique copy AND a unique title. Every other ad in your set is competing against itself — this one isn't. We'd duplicate it into 3 ad sets with different audience signals (broad, interest, lookalike) and shift 60% of budget to it for 7 days while you ship the replacements below.

ad_to_kill.headline: New Arrivals
ad_to_kill.body: Evening elegance, reimagined. Discover the latest eveningwear from SS26.
ad_to_kill.why: It's a verbatim copy of two other live ads. Meta is forced to pick a winner between three identical creatives — and the other two get throttled. Killing this one recovers ~14% of the daily spend Meta is silently consolidating away from it.

concepts.hook: Why we still pin every seam by hand.
concepts.primary_text: The Roland Mouret cut isn't software-generated — it's pinned, draped, and re-pinned on a body, the way it was when the brand started. SS26's structured separates carry that same hand. See the new collection.
concepts.visual_direction: Close-up of fabric being pinned on a dress form, hands in frame, natural light, zero text overlay for first 3 seconds — the gesture is the hook.
concepts.fills_gap: Founder/craft angle. Zero of your current ads use this — and it's the strongest position for a luxury brand against fast-fashion clones.

concepts.hook: One dress. Boardroom at 9. Dinner at 8.
concepts.primary_text: Most evening pieces sit in your closet 50 weeks a year. SS26 was cut so you don't have to choose. Tap to see how three pieces transition from day to night.
concepts.visual_direction: Card 1 — same model, daytime styling, blazer over. Card 2 — accessories swap. Card 3 — evening styling, jewelry, hair down. Card 4 — product shot with 'Shop the look' CTA.
concepts.fills_gap: Carousel format (you have zero). Versatility angle directly counters the perception that eveningwear is single-occasion — opens up your TAM.

concepts.concept_name: What She Said When She Got It
concepts.hook: I didn't expect it to fit like this.
concepts.primary_text: When eveningwear arrives in the mail, you brace for disappointment. SS26 was cut to fit on the body, not on a hanger. Hear what real customers said when they tried it on for the first time.
concepts.visual_direction: Three real customers, unboxing → trying on → genuine reaction. No script, no music for the first 5 seconds. Authentic phone-shot quality.
concepts.fills_gap: UGC + social proof. You have neither in any current ad. UGC creative typically scales 2.4x further than studio creative in fashion at your AOV tier.

unlocks: Format diversity goes from 1 to 4 (image, video, carousel, UGC) — Meta's optimizer can finally find a winner
Hook diversity goes from 3 to 6 unique angles — each speaks to a different buyer state
Auction self-cannibalization drops to ~0% — every dollar of spend works for one creative
Estimated creative half-life lifts from 12 → 24 days, putting you at category median

next_step.urgency: 4 of your ads die in the next 8 days
next_step.headline: Let's get on a 30-min call this week.
next_step.body: I'll walk you through the full briefs live — shot list for The Tailor's Bench, the UGC creator brief, the carousel layout in Figma — and you can decide if we're a fit while we're on the call. If we are, we can have the first replacement ad in your account before Ad #1 dies.

method_note: Built from Roland Mouret's 7 live Meta ads via the Meta Ad Library. No internal data accessed. Days-until-fatigue is a model estimate based on creative diversity signals and category benchmarks — directional, not exact.
